mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy-rate trend is upward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 5.33 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if upward 3-month FEDFUNDS trends are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a low-cycle threshold signals labor-market deterioration and falling earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 3.8% and is higher than 3 months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 3.9 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if this UNRATE crossing condition is followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is already restrictive, investors price a longer high-rate regime, compressing equity multiples and generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 307.619 and FEDFUNDS was 5.33 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate high-inflation condition produces average 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in RSAFS indicates resilient household demand and supports revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 704954 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS trends have a 20-day forward hit rate below 50% or an average return not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening construction activity and rate-sensitive demand, leading analysts to reduce cyclical earnings forecasts and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1358.0 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of historical walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving real output and operating leverage, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 103.6115 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends fail to produce average 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Strong RSAFS growth while FEDFUNDS is above 5% signals demand resilience that delays policy easing, so the discount-rate effect dominates revenue strength and causes negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 704954 and FEDFUNDS was 5.33 in the latest available monthly snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition has a positive 20-day forward return hit rate above 55% or an average return higher than periods with FEDFUNDS below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth paired with UNRATE at or below 4% confirms a soft-landing labor backdrop, supporting income growth and risk appetite, and producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150 thousand and UNRATE <= 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 156923.0 and UNRATE was 3.9 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if this labor-strength condition is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.
